It can be streamed using well-defined interfaces, or it can be stored and accessed from data sources. 8.1.2.1 Streaming interfaces are defined in other SEMI Standards (e.g., SECS/GEM, EDA, etc.), other industry standards, or custom interfaces. 8.1.2.2 Data sources are locations or systems that store and manage data on the manufacturing equipment's computing devices. 8.1.2.2.1 Data sources include, but are not limited to, files (e.g., log files capturing details about operations and activities, files capturing result details, or files with configuration details) and databases (usually large collections of data that are organized for rapid search and retrieval). 8.1.2.2.2 Local data sources are stored on computing devices that are part of the manufacturing equipment. 8.1.2.2.3 Remote data sources are stored on computing devices separate from the manufacturing equipment (e.g., an analysis server located on the factory network). 9 Shareable and Nonshareable Data 9.1 For the purpose of this Specification, equipment data is grouped into two classifications: shareable and nonshareable.

SEMI E191-1024 SEMI 2024 semr 7 Overview 7.1 There are many components that contribute to a computing device's cybersecurity. They include, but are not limited to, the operating system (OS) and installed patches, installed application software and patches, and how the computing device is configured (e.g., what services are running and what network pons are accessible). 7.2 Equipment users determine what cybersecurity elements are important to them based on their risk assessment. The equipment user can use a computing device's cybersecurity status information to evaluate if the computing device satisfies their minimum threshold.
